brace/bracket balance\n397\t        for ch in line.chars() {\n398\t            match ch {\n399\t                '{' => self.open_braces += 1,\n400\t                '}' => self.open_braces = self.open_braces.saturating_sub(1),\n401\t                '[' => self.open_brackets += 1,\n402\t                ']' => self.open_brackets = self.open_brackets.saturating_sub(1),\n403\t                _ => {}\n404\t            }\n405\t        }\n406\t\n407\t        // Track nested levels for duplicate key checking\n408\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n409\t        if let Some(colon_pos) =            let level = indent / 2;\n414\t\n415\t                // Ensure we have enough levels\n416\t                while self.keys_at_level.len() <= level {\n417\t                    self.keys_at_level.push(Vec::new());\n418\t                }\n419\t\n420\t                // Check for duplicates\n421\t                if self.keys_at_level[level].contains(&key.to_string()) {\n422\t YAML syntax error detection module\n//!\n//! This module provides comprehensive syntax validation for YAML content,\n//! detecting common errors including indentation problems, delimiter issues,\n//! and structure violations.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::types::{ValidationError, ValidationResult};\nuse std::collections::VecDeque;\n\n/// Syntax validator for YAML content\n///\n/// This struct performs line-by-line analysis of YAML content to detect\n/// common syntax errors and structural problems.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SyntaxValidator {\n    /// Whether to use strict indentation checking (disallow tabs)\n    strict_indentation: bool,\n    /// Whether to check for duplicate keys\n    check_duplicate_keys: bool,\n    /// Whether to validate block scalar indicators\n    validate_block_scalars: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SyntaxValidator {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_indentation: true,\n            check_duplicate_keys: true,\n            validate_block_scalars: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SyntaxValidator {\n    /// Create a new syntax validator with default settings\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new syntax validator with strict indentation checking\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_indentation: true,\n            check_duplicate_keys: true,\n            validate_block_scalars: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new lenient syntax validator\n    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_indentation: false,\n            check_duplicate_keys: false,\n            validate_block_scalars: false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content to validate\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ValidationResult containing any errors or warnings found\n    pub fn validate(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n\n        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n        let mut context = ValidationContext::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1indexed = line_num + 1;\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comment-only lines for most checks\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Run each validation pass\n            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_indentation(line, line_num_1indexed, &context) {\n                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n            }\n\n            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_delimiters(line, line_num_1indexed) {\n                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n            }\n\n            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_structure(line, line_num_1indexed, &context) {\n                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n            }\n\n            // Update context for next line\n            context.update_from_line(line, line_num_1indexed);\n        }\n\n        // Final validation checks\n        if let Err(mut final_errors) = self.validate_final_structure(&context) {\n            errors.append(&mut final_errors);\n        }\n\n        ValidationResult {\n            valid: errors.is_empty(),\n            errors,\n            warnings,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate indentation on a single line\n    fn validate_indentation(\n        &self,\n        line: &str,\n        line_num: usize,\n        context: &ValidationContext,\n    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for tabs in strict mode\n        if self.strict_indentation && line.contains('\\t') {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                \"tabs are not allowed in YAML (use spaces for indentation)\"\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        // Check for mixed indentation (tabs and spaces)\n        let leading_bytes = line.as_bytes();\n        let mut has_tabs = false;\n        let mut has_spaces = false;\n\n        for &byte in leading_bytes.iter().take_while(|&&b| b == b' ' || b == b'\\t') {\n            if byte == b'\\t' {\n                has_tabs = true;\n            } else if byte == b' ' {\n                has_spaces = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                \"mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\"\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        // Check indentation consistency\n        let current_indent = self.count_indentation(line);\n        if let Some(prev_indent) = context.last_indentation {\n            // Check for indentation that's not a multiple of 2 (common convention)\n            if self.strict_indentation && current_indent % 2 != 0 && current_indent > 0 {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    &format!(\"indentation of {} spaces is not a multiple of 2\", current_indent)\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n\n            // Check for dramatic indentation increases (more than 2 levels at once)\n            if current_indent > prev_indent + 4 {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    &format!(\"indentation increased by {} levels (should increase by at most 2)\",\n                             current_indent - prev_indent)\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate delimiters on a single line\n    fn validate_delimiters(\n        &self,\n        line: &str,\n        line_num: usize,\n    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for flow mapping/sequence delimiter balance\n        let mut brace_stack = VecDeque::new();\n        let mut bracket_stack = VecDeque::new();\n\n        for (col, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n            match ch {\n                '{' => brace_stack.push_front(('{', col)),\n                '}' => {\n                    if brace_stack.pop_front().is_none() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                            \"unmatched closing brace '}'\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n                '[' => bracket_stack.push_front(('[', col)),\n                ']' => {\n                    if bracket_stack.pop_front().is_none() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                            \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n                ':' => {\n                    // Check if colon is used as key-value separator\n                    // (followed by space or end of line)\n                    let next_char = line.chars().nth(col + 1);\n                    if next_char.map_or(false, |c| !c.is_whitespace() && c != ':') {\n                        // This might be a value part (like in \"http://\")\n                        // but if it's at the end of non-whitespace, it's suspicious\n                        let remaining = &line[col..];\n                        if remaining.trim_matches(':').is_empty() || remaining.trim().starts_with('#') {\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                                \"key-value colon without value\"\n                            ).with_line(line_num));\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Report unmatched opening delimiters\n        for (delimiter, col) in brace_stack {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening brace '{{' at column {}\", col + 1)\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        for (delimiter, col) in bracket_stack {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening bracket '[' at column {}\", col + 1)\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate structure on a single line\n    fn validate_structure(\n        &self,\n        line: &str,\n        line_num: usize,\n        context: &ValidationContext,\n    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Check for invalid document start marker\n        if trimmed == \"---\" || line.trim_start().starts_with(\"---\") {\n            if !line.trim_start().starts_with(\"--- \") && trimmed != \"---\" {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    \"document start marker '---' must be followed by space or be on its own line\"\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for invalid document end marker\n        if trimmed == \"...\" || line.trim_start().starts_with(\"...\") {\n            if !line.trim_start().starts_with(\"... \") && trimmed != \"...\" {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    \"document end marker '...' must be followed by space or be on its own line\"\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for block scalar indicators\n        if self.validate_block_scalars {\n            if trimmed.starts_with('|') || trimmed.starts_with('>') {\n                let indicator = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n                // Check if indicator is followed by valid modifier or space\n                let rest = &trimmed[1..];\n                if !rest.is_empty() && !rest.starts_with(' ') && !rest.starts_with('-') && !rest.starts_with('+') {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                        &format!(\"block scalar indicator '{}' must be followed by space, '+', '-', or end of line\", indicator)\n                    ).with_line(line_num));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for anchor and alias definitions\n        if trimmed.contains('&') {\n            for (col, _) in line.match_indices('&') {\n                // Check if anchor name is valid\n                let rest = &line[col + 1..];\n                let rest_trimmed = rest.trim();\n\n                // If there's nothing after the &, or only whitespace, it's invalid\n                if rest_trimmed.is_empty() {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                        \"anchor '&' must be followed by a name\"\n                    ).with_line(line_num));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if trimmed.contains('*') {\n            for (col, _) in line.match_indices('*') {\n                let rest = &line[col + 1..];\n                let rest_trimmed = rest.trim();\n\n                // If there's nothing after the *, or only whitespace, it's invalid\n                if rest_trimmed.is_empty() {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                        \"alias '*' must be followed by a name\"\n                    ).with_line(line_num));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Final structure validation after processing all lines\n    fn validate_final_structure(&self, context: &ValidationContext) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for unbalanced flow containers across the document\n        if context.open_braces > 0 {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"document\",\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening brace(s) - {} unclosed\", context.open_braces)\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if context.open_brackets > 0 {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"document\",\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening bracket(s) - {} unclosed\", context.open_brackets)\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Count the indentation level of a line\n    fn count_indentation(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n        let leading_spaces = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();\n        let leading_tabs = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '\\t').count();\n\n        if leading_tabs > 0 {\n            // Tabs count as 8 spaces (traditional tab width)\n            leading_spaces + (leading_tabs * 8)\n        } else {\n            leading_spaces\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validation context maintained during line-by-line processing\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct ValidationContext {\n    /// Last indentation level seen\n    last_indentation: Option<usize>,\n    /// Number of currently open braces\n    open_braces: usize,\n    /// Number of currently open brackets\n    open_brackets: usize,\n    /// Keys seen at current indentation level\n    keys_at_level: Vec<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationContext {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    fn update_from_line(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize) {\n        let indent = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ' || c == '\\t').count();\n        self.last_indentation = Some(indent);\n\n        // Track brace/bracket balance\n        for ch in line.chars() {\n            match ch {\n                '{' => self.open_braces += 1,\n                '}' => self.open_braces = self.open_braces.saturating_sub(1),\n                '[' => self.open_brackets += 1,\n                ']' => self.open_brackets = self.open_brackets.saturating_sub(1),\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Track nested levels for duplicate key checking\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n            let key = trimmed[..colon_pos].trim();\n            if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('#') {\n                // Determine nesting level\n                let level = indent / 2;\n\n                // Ensure we have enough levels\n                while self.keys_at_level.len() <= level {\n                    self.keys_at_level.push(Vec::new());\n                }\n\n                // Check for duplicates\n                if self.keys_at_level[level].contains(&key.to_string()) {\n                    // Would add a warning about duplicate key\n                } else {\n                    self.keys_at_level[level].push(key.to_string());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_empty_content() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let result = validator.validate(\"\");\n        assert!(result.is_valid());\n        assert!(result.errors.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_simple_valid_yaml() {\n        let validator =   #[test]\n    fn test_detect_tabs_in_strict_mode() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::strict();\n        let yaml = \"name:\\tvalue\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n        assert!(result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"tabs\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let yaml = \"\\t name: value\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n        assert!(result.errors.iter().any(|e| This module provides comprehensive syntax error detection for YAML files.\n4\t//! It detects three main categories of errors:\n5\t//!\n6\t//! 1. **Indentation Errors**: Mixed spaces/tabs, inconsistent indentation levels\n7\t//! 2. **Delimiter Errors**: Missing colons, unbalanced brackets/braces, quote errors\n8\t//! 3. **Structure Errors**: Invalid mappings, malformed sequences, duplicate keys\n9\t//!\n10\t//! ## Architecture\n11\t//!\n12\t//! /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n217\t    ///\n218\t    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. ///\n224\t    /// # Returns\n225\t    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n226\t    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n227\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n228\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n229\t\n230\t        // Reset state for new detection\n231\t        self.reset_state();\n232\t\n233\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n234\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n235\t\n236\t            //            self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n255\t                }\n256\t            }\n257\t        }\n258\t\n259\t        // Final validation checks\n260\t        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n261\t        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n262\t\n263\t        errors\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n267\t    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n268\t        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n269\t\n270\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n271\t            ValidationResult::success()\n272\t        } else {\n273\t            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n274\t        }\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    /// Reset all detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n286\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n287\t\n288\t        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n289\t        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n290\t            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n291\t            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n292\t            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n293\t\n294\t            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n295\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n296\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n297\t                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n298\t\n299\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n300\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n301\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n302\t                    error_type.description()\n303\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n304\t            } else if has_tabs {\n305\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n306\t                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n307\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n308\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n309\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n310\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n311\t                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n312\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n313\t                }\n314\t            } else if has_spaces {\n315\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n316\t            }\n317\t        }\n318\t\n319\t        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n320\t        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n321\t            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n322\t\n323\t            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n324\t            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n325\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n326\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n327\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n328\t                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n329\t                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n330\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n331\t            }\n332\t\n333\t            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n334\t            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n335\t                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n336\t\n337\t                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n338\t                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n339\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n340\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n341\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n342\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n343\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n344\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n345\t                }\n346\t\n347\t                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n348\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n349\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n350\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n351\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n352\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n353\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n354\t                } else {\n355\t                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n356\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n357\t                }\n358\t            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n359\t                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n360\t                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n361\t                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n362\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n363\t                }\n364\t            }\n365\t\n366\t            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n367\t        }\n368\t    }\n369\t\n370\t    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n371\t    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n372\t        // Check for missing colons after keys\n373\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n374\t        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n375\t           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n376\t           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n377\t           !trimmed.starts_with('#') && // Not a comment\n378\t           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n379\t           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n380\t           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n381\t           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n382\t           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n383\t\n384\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n385\t                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n386\t                \"missing colon after key\"\n387\t            ).with_line(line_num));\n388\t        }\n389\t\n390\t        // Track bracket/brace balance\n391\t        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n392\t            match ch {\n393\t                '[' => {\n394\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n395\t                }\n396\t                ']' => {\n397\t                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n398\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n399\t                    } else {\n400\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n401\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n402\t                            \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\"\n403\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n404\t                    }\n405\t                }\n406\t                '{' => {\n407\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n408\t                }\n409\t                '}' => {\n410\t                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n411\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n412\t                    } else {\n413\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n414\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n415\t                            \"unmatched closing brace '}'\"\n416\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n417\t                    }\n418\t                }\n419\t                '\\'' => {\n420\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n421\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n422\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n423\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n424\t                            // Mismatched quote\n425\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n426\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n427\t                                \"mismatched quotes (single inside          }\n432\t                }\n433\t                '\"' => {\n434\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n435\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n436\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n437\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n438\t                            // Mismatched quote\n439\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n440\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n441\t                                \"mismatched quotes (double inside single)\"\n442\t                            ).with_line(line_num));\n443\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n444\t                        }\n445\t                    }\n446\t                }\n447\t                _ => {}\n448\t            }\n449\t        }\n450\t    }\n451\t\n452\t    /// Detect structure-related errors\n453\t    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n454\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n455\t\n456\t        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n457\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n458\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n459\t                // Valid sequence item\n460\t                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n461\t                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n462\t                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n463\t                }\n464\t            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n465\t                // Dash followed by something other than space\n466\t                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n467\t                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n468\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n469\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n470\t                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n471\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n472\t                    }\n473\t                }\n474\t            }\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n478\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n479\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n480\t                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n481\t\n482\t                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n483\t                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n484\t                    // Valid key: value pair\n485\t                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n486\t                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n487\t                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n488\t                    }\n489\t                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n490\t                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n491\t                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n492\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n493\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n494\t                            \"colon ':' at      StructureContext::FlowMapping\n505\t            } else {\n506\t                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n507\t            });\n508\t        }\n509\t    }\n510\t\n511\t    /// Detect duplicate key errors\n512\t    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n513\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n514\t        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n515\t\n516\t        // Check if we're moving to a new context (indentation decreased)\n517\t        // This indicates we've exited a nested }\n522\t\n523\t        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n524\t        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n525\t\n526\t        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n527\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n528\t            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n529\t\n530\t            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n531\t            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n532\t            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n533\t            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n534\t                                after_colon.starts_with('#') ||\n535\t                                (colon_pos > 0 && !key_part.contains(':'));\n536\t\n537\t            if is_parent_key {\n538\t                // Clear current keys when we encounter a new parent key\n539\t                self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n540\t                return;\n541\t            }\n542\t\n543\t            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n544\t            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n545\t                return;\n546\t            }\n547\t\n548\t            let key = key_part.trim();\n549\t\n550\t            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n551\t            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n552\t                return;\n553\t            }\n554\t\n555\t            // Check for duplicates at this indentation level\n556\t            if self.structure_state.current_keys.contains(key) {\n557\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n558\t                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n559\t                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' at same indentation level\", key)\n560\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n561\t            } else {\n562\t                self.structure_state.current_keys.insert(key.to_string());\n563\t            }\n564\t\n565\t            // Track all keys for global duplicate detection\n566\t            self.structure_state.all_keys\n567\t                .entry(key.to_string())\n568\t                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n569\t                .push(line_num);\n570\t        }\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n574\t    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n575\t        // Check for unclosed brackets\n576\t        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n577\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n578\t                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n579\t                format!(\"unclosed '{}'\", bracket)\n580\t            ).with_line(*line_num));\n581\t        }\n582\t\n583\t        // Check for unclosed quotes\n584\t        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n585\t            let quote_type = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n586\t                Some(QuoteType::Single) => \"'\",\n587\t                Some(QuoteType::Double) => \"\\\"\",\n588\t                None => \"\",\n589\t            };\n590\t\n591\t            if !quote_type.is_empty() {\n592\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n593\t                    \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n594\t                    format!(\"unclosed quote '{}'\", quote_type)\n595\t                ).with_line(0)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n596\t            }\n597\t        }\n598\t    }\n599\t\n600\t    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n601\t    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n602\t        // Check for global duplicate keys\n603\t        for (key, line_nums) in &self.structure_state.all_keys {\n604\t            if line_nums.len() > 1 {\n605\t                // Only report if not at same indentation level (already reported)\n606\t                let is_same_level = line_nums.windows(2).all(|w| w[0] == w[1]);\n607\t                if !is_same_level {\n608\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n609\t                        format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n610\t                        format!(\"duplicate key '{}' appears {} times in document\", key, line_nums.len())\n611\t                    ).with_line(line_nums[0]));\n612\t                }\n613\t            }\n614\t        }\n615\t    }\n616\t\n617\t    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n618\t    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n619\t        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n620\t    }\n621\t\n622\t    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n623\t    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n624\t        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n625\t        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n626\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n627\t            return false;\n628\t        }\n629\t\n630\t        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n631\t\n632\t        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n633\t        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n634\t            return false;\n635\t        }\n636\t\n637\t        // Look for word characters at the start\n638\t        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n639\t    }\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\timpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n643\t    fn default() -> Self {\n644\t        Self::new()\n645\t    }\n646\t}\n647\t\n648\t#[cfg(test)]\n649\tmod tests {\n650\t    use super::*;\n651\t\n652\t    #[test]\n653\t    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n659\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n660\t    }\n661\t\n662\t    #[test]\n663\t    fn test_detect_missing_colon() YAML Syntax Error Detection Module\n//!\n//! This module provides comprehensive syntax error detection for YAML files.\n//! It detects three main categories of errors:\n//!\n//! 1. **Indentation Errors**: Mixed spaces/tabs, inconsistent indentation levels\n//! 2. **Delimiter Errors**: Missing colons, unbalanced brackets/braces, quote errors\n//! 3. **Structure Errors**: Invalid mappings, malformed sequences, duplicate keys\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! The detector follows a line-by-line analysis approach:\n//!\n//! - LineTracker: Tracks line numbers and content for error reporting\n//! - IndentationDetector: Analyzes indentation patterns\n//! - DelimiterDetector: Checks delimiter consistency\n//! - StructureDetector: Validates YAML structure rules\n//!\n//! ## Usage\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::{SyntaxDetector, SyntaxError};\n//!\n//! let detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n//! let yaml_content = \"key: value\\n  nested: value\";\n//! let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml_content);\n//!\n//! for error in errors {\n//!     println!(\"Line {}: {}\", error.line, error.message);\n//! }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::types::{ValidationError, ValidationResult};\nuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Classification of indentation error types\n///\n/// This enum provides structured categorization of different indentation\n/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum IndentationErrorType {\n    /// Mixed tabs and spaces in the same line\n    MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n    /// Indentation not a multiple of the base indent size\n    InvalidIndentLevel,\n    /// Indentation increase is too large (> 2 levels at once)\n    ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n    /// Indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\n    InvalidIndentIncrease,\n    /// Tab character detected (when tabs are not allowed)\n    TabCharacter,\n}\n\nimpl IndentationErrorType {\n    /// Get a human-readable description of this error type\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"indentation level is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is too large\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n            Self::TabCharacter => \"tab character detected in indentation\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a short code for this error type\n    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"E001\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"E002\",\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"E003\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"E004\",\n            Self::TabCharacter => \"E005\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all indentation error types\n    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n        &[\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel,\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease,\n            Self::TabCharacter,\n        ]\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for IndentationErrorType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Main syntax detector for YAML content\n///\n/// This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n/// a unified interface for error detection.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SyntaxDetector {\n    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n    config: DetectorConfig,\n    /// Track indentation state across lines\n    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n    structure_state: StructureState,\n}\n\n/// Configuration for syntax detection behavior\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DetectorConfig {\n    /// Whether to detect tab/space mixing\n    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n            base_indent_size: 2,\n            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct IndentationState {\n    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n    has_tabs: bool,\n    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n    has_spaces: bool,\n    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n    prev_indent_level: usize,\n    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n}\n\n/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct DelimiterState {\n    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n    /// Lines with quote errors\n    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n}\n\n/// Quote type for tracking\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum QuoteType {\n    Single,\n    Double,\n}\n\n/// State tracking for structure analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct StructureState {\n    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n    /// Keys seen at current indentation level\n    current_keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// All keys seen in the document (for duplicate detection)\n    all_keys: HashMap<String, Vec<usize>>,\n    /// Previous line's indentation level\n    prev_indent: usize,\n}\n\n/// Current structure context\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum StructureContext {\n    Mapping,\n    Sequence,\n    FlowMapping,\n    FlowSequence,\n}\n\nimpl SyntaxDetector {\n    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n    ///\n    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n\n        // Reset state for new detection\n        self.reset_state();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n\n            if is_meaningful {\n                // Run all enabled detectors\n                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Final validation checks\n        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n\n        errors\n    }\n\n    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            ValidationResult::success()\n        } else {\n            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n    }\n\n    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n\n            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n\n                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                    error_type.description()\n                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n            } else if has_tabs {\n                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                }\n            } else if has_spaces {\n                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n\n            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n            }\n\n            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n\n                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                }\n\n                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                } else {\n                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n                }\n            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Check for missing colons after keys\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n           !trimmed.starts_with('#') && // Not a comment\n           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                \"missing colon after key\"\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        // Track bracket/brace balance\n        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n            match ch {\n                '[' => {\n                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n                }\n                ']' => {\n                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n                    } else {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n                '{' => {\n                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n                }\n                '}' => {\n                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n                    } else {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"unmatched closing brace '}'\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n                '\\'' => {\n                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n                            // Mismatched quote\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                                \"mismatched quotes (single inside double)\"\n                            ).with_line(line_num));\n                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                '\"' => {\n                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n                            // Mismatched quote\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                                \"mismatched quotes (double inside single)\"\n                            ).with_line(line_num));\n                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect structure-related errors\n    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                // Valid sequence item\n                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n                }\n            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n                // Dash followed by something other than space\n                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n\n                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n                    // Valid key: value pair\n                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n                    }\n                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for flow syntax indicators\n        if trimmed.contains('{') || trimmed.contains('[') {\n            self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n            } else {\n                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate key errors\n    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Check if we're moving to a new context (indentation decreased)\n        // This indicates we've exited a nested structure\n        if indent < self.structure_state.prev_indent {\n            // Clear keys when we exit a context\n            self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n        }\n\n        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n\n        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n\n            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n                                after_colon.starts_with('#') ||\n                                (colon_pos > 0 && !key_part.contains(':'));\n\n            if is_parent_key {\n                // Clear current keys when we encounter a new parent key\n                self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            let key = key_part.trim();\n\n            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Check for duplicates at this indentation level\n            if self.structure_state.current_keys.contains(key) {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' at same indentation level\", key)\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            } else {\n                self.structure_state.current_keys.insert(key.to_string());\n            }\n\n            // Track all keys for global duplicate detection\n            self.structure_state.all_keys\n                .entry(key.to_string())\n                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                .push(line_num);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Check for unclosed brackets\n        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n                format!(\"unclosed '{}'\", bracket)\n            ).with_line(*line_num));\n        }\n\n        // Check for unclosed quotes\n        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n            let quote_type = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                Some(QuoteType::Single) => \"'\",\n                Some(QuoteType::Double) => \"\\\"\",\n                None => \"\",\n            };\n\n            if !quote_type.is_empty() {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n                    format!(\"unclosed quote '{}'\", quote_type)\n                ).with_line(0)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Check for global duplicate keys\n        for (key, line_nums) in &self.structure_state.all_keys {\n            if line_nums.len() > 1 {\n                // Only report if not at same indentation level (already reported)\n                let is_same_level = line_nums.windows(2).all(|w| w[0] == w[1]);\n                if !is_same_level {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n                        format!(\"duplicate key '{}' appears {} times in document\", key, line_nums.len())\n                    ).with_line(line_nums[0]));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n\n        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Look for word characters at the start\n        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: value\\n\\t  bad: value\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_missing_colon() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key value\\n  key2: value2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"missing colon\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: [value1, value2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"unclosed\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_valid_yaml_no_errors() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: value\\n  nested:\\n    - item1\\n    - item2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(errors.is_empty());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":691,"startLine":1,"totalLines":691}}}
