{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c998d6f42f0543ecbb929c94","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result types for YAML parsing operations\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the result types used by the YAML parser.\n4\t\n5\tuse crate::parsers::yaml::error::{ParseError, Result};\n6\tuse std::fmt;\n7\t\n8\t/// Status enum representing success/error states for Result types\n9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n10\tpub enum Status {\n11\t    /// Operation completed successfully\n12\t    SUCCESS,\n13\t    /// Operation encountered an error\n14\t    ERROR,\n15\t}\n16\t\n17\timpl Status {\n18\t    /// Check if status is SUCCESS\n19\t    pub fn is_success(self) -> bool {\n20\t        matches!(self, Status::SUCCESS)\n21\t    }\n22\t\n23\t    /// Check if status is ERROR\n24\t    pub fn is_error(self) -> bool {\n25\t        matches!(self, Status::ERROR)\n26\t    }\n27\t\n28\t    /// Convert from boolean (true = SUCCESS, false = ERROR)\n29\t    pub fn from_bool(success: bool) -> Self {\n30\t        if success {\n31\t            Status::SUCCESS\n32\t        } else {\n33\t            Status::ERROR\n34\t        }\n35\t    }\n36\t\n37\t    /// Convert to boolean\n38\t    pub fn as_bool(self) -> bool {\n39\t        self.is_success()\n40\t    }\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\timpl fmt::Display for Status {\n44\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n45\t        match self {\n46\t            Status::SUCCESS => write!(f, \"SUCCESS\"),\n47\t            Status::ERROR => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n48\t        }\n49\t    }\n50\t}\n51\t\n52\t/// Result of a YAML operation with status, data, and error fields\n53\t///\n54\t/// This is a simple dataclass-style result type that holds:\n55\t/// - `status`: A Status enum indicating SUCCESS or ERROR\n56\t/// - `data`: Optional generic data payload\n57\t/// - `error`: Optional error message string\n58\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n59\tpub struct OperationResult<T> {\n60\t    /// The operation status (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n61\t    pub status: Status,\n62\t    /// The parsed content data, if successful\n63\t    pub data: Option<T>,\n64\t    /// The error message, if the operation failed\n65\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\timpl<T> OperationResult<T> {\n69\t    /// Create a new OperationResult with all three fields\n70\t    ///\n71\t    /// # Arguments\n72\t    /// * `status` - The Status enum value (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n73\t    /// * `data` - Optional data payload\n74\t    /// * `error` - Optional error message\n75\t    pub fn new(status: Status, data: Option<T>, error: Option<String>) -> Self {\n76\t        Self { status, data, error }\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    /// Create a successful OperationResult with data\n80\t    ///\n81\t    /// # Arguments\n82\t    /// * `data` - The successful result data\n83\t    pub fn success(data: T) -> Self {\n84\t        Self {\n85\t            status: Status::SUCCESS,\n86\t            data: Some(data),\n87\t            error: None,\n88\t        }\n89\t    }\n90\t\n91\t    /// Create a failed OperationResult with an error message\n92\t    ///\n93\t    /// # Arguments\n94\t    /// * `message` - The error message describing the failure\n95\t    pub fn error(message: String) -> Self {\n96\t        Self {\n97\t            status: Status::ERROR,\n98\t            data: None,\n99\t            error: Some(message),\n100\t        }\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Check if the operation was successful\n104\t    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n105\t        self.status.is_success()\n106\t    }\n107\t\n108\t    /// Check if the operation failed\n109\t    pub fn is_error(&self) -> bool {\n110\t        self.status.is_error()\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    /// Get a reference to the data, if successful\n114\t    pub fn get_data(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n115\t        self.data.as_ref()\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Get the error message as a string slice, if failed\n119\t    pub fn get_error(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n120\t        self.error.as_deref()\n121\t    }\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Result of a YAML parsing operation\n125\t///\n126\t/// `ParseResult<T>` represents the outcome of a YAML parsing operation, encapsulating\n127\t/// either a successful parse with its associated data and metadata, or a failure with\n128\t/// detailed error information.\n129\t///\n130\t/// # Type Parameter\n131\t///\n132\t/// - `T` - The type of the parsed value. This is typically the target type that the YAML\n133\t///   content is being parsed into (e.g., a configuration struct, `serde_yaml::Value`,\n134\t///   or any deserializable type).\n135\t///\n136\t/// # Fields\n137\t///\n138\t/// ## Core Fields\n139\t///\n140\t/// - `value: Option<T>` - The successfully parsed value. Present when parsing succeeds.\n141\t/// - `error: Option<ParseError>` - Detailed error information. Present when parsing fails.\n142\t/// - `metadata: ParseMetadata` - Metadata about the parsing operation (lines processed,\n143\t///   bytes processed, processing time, source path). Always present.\n144\t/// - `warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>` - Collection of non-fatal warnings that occurred during\n145\t///   parsing. Parsing can succeed with warnings present.\n146\t///\n147\t/// # Design Philosophy\n148\t///\n149\t/// `ParseResult<T>` follows a structured approach to representing parsing outcomes:\n150\t///\n151\t/// 1. **Explicit success/failure states** - Success requires both `value` present and\n152\t///    `error` absent. Failure requires `error` present and `value` absent.\n153\t/// 2. **Rich context** - Both success and failure carry metadata about the operation.\n154\t/// 3. **Warnings without failure** - Warnings are non-fatal; parsing can succeed with\n155\t///    warnings present (e.g., deprecated field usage, unknown keys in lenient mode).\n156\t/// 4. **Composable operations** - Supports `map()` for transforming success values,\n157\t///    and `From<Result<T>>` for interoperability with standard `Result` types.\n158\t///\n159\t/// # Examples\n160\t///\n161\t/// ## Successful Parse\n162\t///\n163\t/// ```ignore\n164\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseMetadata};\n165\t///\n166\t/// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n167\t/// assert!(result.is_success());\n168\t/// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n169\t/// assert!(result.warnings().is_empty());\n170\t/// ```\n171\t///\n172\t/// ## Failed Parse\n173\t///\n174\t/// ```ignore\n175\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n176\t///\n177\t/// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n178\t/// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n179\t/// assert!(result.is_failure());\n180\t/// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n181\t/// ```\n182\t///\n183\t/// ## Parse with Warnings\n184\t///\n185\t/// ```ignore\n186\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n187\t///\n188\t/// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n189\t/// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_field\", \"new_field\"));\n190\t///\n191\t/// assert!(result.is_success());\n192\t/// assert!(!result.warnings().is_empty());\n193\t/// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n194\t/// ```\n195\t///\n196\t/// ## Mapping Over Success\n197\t///\n198\t/// ```ignore\n199\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n200\t///\n201\t/// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n202\t/// let mapped = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n203\t///\n204\t/// assert_eq!(mapped.value(), Some(&20));\n205\t/// ```\n206\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n207\tpub struct ParseResult<T> {\n208\t    /// The parsed value, if successful\n209\t    value: Option<T>,\n210\t    /// The error, if parsing failed\n211\t    error: Option<ParseError>,\n212\t    /// Additional metadata about the parse operation\n213\t    metadata: ParseMetadata,\n214\t    /// Non-fatal warnings that occurred during parsing\n215\t    warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>,\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\timpl<T> ParseResult<T> {\n219\t    /// Create a successful parse result\n220\t    ///\n221\t    /// # Arguments\n222\t    /// * `value` - The successfully parsed value\n223\t    ///\n224\t    /// # Returns\n225\t    /// A `ParseResult` with the value set, no error, empty warnings, and default metadata\n226\t    ///\n227\t    /// # Examples\n228\t    ///\n229\t    /// ```\n230\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n231\t    ///\n232\t    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n233\t    /// assert!(result.is_success());\n234\t    /// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n235\t    /// ```\n236\t    pub fn success(value: T) -> Self {\n237\t        Self {\n238\t            value: Some(value),\n239\t            error: None,\n240\t            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n241\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n242\t        }\n243\t    }\n244\t\n245\t    /// Create a failed parse result\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// # Arguments\n248\t    /// * `error` - The error that caused parsing to fail\n249\t    ///\n250\t    /// # Returns\n251\t    /// A `ParseResult` with the error set, no value, empty warnings, and default metadata\n252\t    ///\n253\t    /// # Examples\n254\t    ///\n255\t    /// ```\n256\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n257\t    ///\n258\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n259\t    /// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n260\t    /// assert!(result.is_failure());\n261\t    /// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n262\t    /// ```\n263\t    pub fn failure(error: ParseError) -> Self {\n264\t        Self {\n265\t            value: None,\n266\t            error: Some(error),\n267\t            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n268\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n269\t        }\n270\t    }\n271\t\n272\t    /// Check if the parse was successful\n273\t    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n274\t        self.error.is_none() && self.value.is_some()\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    /// Check if the parse failed\n278\t    pub fn is_failure(&self) -> bool {\n279\t        self.error.is_some()\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    /// Get the parsed value\n283\t    ///\n284\t    /// Returns None if the parse failed\n285\t    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n286\t        self.value.as_ref()\n287\t    }\n288\t\n289\t    /// Get the error, if any\n290\t    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&ParseError> {\n291\t        self.error.as_ref()\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    /// Get the metadata for this parse result\n295\t    ///\n296\t    /// # Returns\n297\t    /// A reference to the `ParseMetadata` containing information about lines processed,\n298\t    /// bytes processed, processing time, and source path\n299\t    pub fn metadata(&self) -> &ParseMetadata {\n300\t        &self.metadata\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    /// Get the warnings for this parse result\n304\t    ///\n305\t    /// # Returns\n306\t    /// A slice of `ParseWarning` items representing non-fatal issues that occurred during parsing\n307\t    ///\n308\t    /// # Examples\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// ```\n311\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n312\t    ///\n313\t    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n314\t    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old\", \"new\"));\n315\t    ///\n316\t    /// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n317\t    /// ```\n318\t    pub fn warnings(&self) -> &[ParseWarning] {\n319\t        &self.warnings\n320\t    }\n321\t\n322\t    /// Check if this parse result has any warnings\n323\t    ///\n324\t    /// # Returns\n325\t    /// `true` if there are warnings present, `false` otherwise\n326\t    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n327\t        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n328\t    }\n329\t\n330\t    /// Add a warning to this parse result\n331\t    ///\n332\t    /// # Arguments\n333\t    /// * `warning` - The warning to add\n334\t    ///\n335\t    /// # Examples\n336\t    ///\n337\t    /// ```\n338\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n339\t    ///\n340\t    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n341\t    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"deprecated_field\"));\n342\t    /// assert!(result.has_warnings());\n343\t    /// ```\n344\t    pub fn add_warning(&mut self, warning: ParseWarning) {\n345\t        self.warnings.push(warning);\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    /// Add warnings to this parse result\n349\t    ///\n350\t    /// # Arguments\n351\t    /// * `warnings` - An iterator of warnings to add\n352\t    pub fn add_warnings<I>(&mut self, warnings: I)\n353\t    where\n354\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = ParseWarning>,\n355\t    {\n356\t        self.warnings.extend(warnings);\n357\t    }\n358\t\n359\t    /// Set the metadata for this parse result\n360\t    ///\n361\t    /// # Arguments\n362\t    /// * `metadata` - The metadata to set\n363\t    pub fn with_metadata(mut self, metadata: ParseMetadata) -> Self {\n364\t        self.metadata = metadata;\n365\t        self\n366\t    }\n367\t\n368\t    /// Unwrap the value, consuming the result\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// # Panics\n371\t    /// Panics if the parse failed (i.e., if `error` is present)\n372\t    ///\n373\t    /// # Examples\n374\t    ///\n375\t    /// ```\n376\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n377\t    ///\n378\t    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n379\t    /// assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), 42);\n380\t    /// ```\n381\t    pub fn unwrap(self) -> T {\n382\t        self.value.expect(\n383\t            \"called unwrap() on a failed ParseResult\"\n384\t        )\n385\t    }\n386\t\n387\t    /// Unwrap the value or return a default\n388\t    ///\n389\t    /// # Arguments\n390\t    /// * `default` - The default value to return if parsing failed\n391\t    ///\n392\t    /// # Returns\n393\t    /// The parsed value if successful, or the provided default\n394\t    ///\n395\t    /// # Examples\n396\t    ///\n397\t    /// ```\n398\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n399\t    ///\n400\t    /// let success: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(42);\n401\t    /// assert_eq!(success.unwrap_or(0), 42);\n402\t    ///\n403\t    /// let failure = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(ParseError::syntax(\"error\"));\n404\t    /// assert_eq!(failure.unwrap_or(0), 0);\n405\t    /// ```\n406\t    pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T {\n407\t        self.value.unwrap_or(default)\n408\t    }\n409\t\n410\t    /// Map the success value to a new type\n411\t    ///\n412\t    /// # Type Parameters\n413\t    /// * `U` - The target type after mapping\n414\t    /// * `F` - Function type that transforms `T` into `U`\n415\t    ///\n416\t    /// # Arguments\n417\t    /// * `f` - A function that transforms the success value\n418\t    ///\n419\t    /// # Returns\n420\t    /// A new `ParseResult<U>` with the transformed value, preserving error, metadata, and warnings\n421\t    ///\n422\t    /// # Examples\n423\t    ///\n424\t    /// ```\n425\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n426\t    ///\n427\t    /// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n428\t    /// let doubled = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n429\t    ///\n430\t    /// assert_eq!(doubled.value(), Some(&20));\n431\t    /// ```\n432\t    pub fn map<U, F>(self, f: F) -> ParseResult<U>\n433\t    where\n434\t        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n435\t    {\n436\t        match self.value {\n437\t            Some(v) => ParseResult {\n438\t                value: Some(f(v)),\n439\t                error: None,\n440\t                metadata: self.metadata,\n441\t                warnings: self.warnings,\n442\t            },\n443\t            None => ParseResult {\n444\t                value: None,\n445\t                error: self.error,\n446\t                metadata: self.metadata,\n447\t                warnings: self.warnings,\n448\t            },\n449\t        }\n450\t    }\n451\t}\n452\t\n453\timpl<T> From<Result<T>> for ParseResult<T> {\n454\t    fn from(result: Result<T>) -> Self {\n455\t        match result {\n456\t            Ok(value) => Self::success(value),\n457\t            Err(error) => Self::failure(error),\n458\t        }\n459\t    }\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\t/// Metadata about a parsing operation\n463\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n464\tpub struct ParseMetadata {\n465\t    /// Number of lines processed\n466\t    pub lines_processed: usize,\n467\t    /// Number of bytes processed\n468\t    pub bytes_processed: usize,\n469\t    /// Processing time in nanoseconds\n470\t    pub processing_time_ns: Option<u64>,\n471\t    /// Source file path, if known\n472\t    pub source_path: Option<String>,\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\timpl ParseMetadata {\n476\t    /// Create new metadata\n477\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n478\t        Self::default()\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    /// Set the number of lines processed\n482\t    pub fn with_lines(mut self, lines: usize) -> Self {\n483\t        self.lines_processed = lines;\n484\t        self\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    /// Set the number of bytes processed\n488\t    pub fn with_bytes(mut self, bytes: usize) -> Self {\n489\t        self.bytes_processed = bytes;\n490\t        self\n491\t    }\n492\t\n493\t    /// Set the source path\n494\t    pub fn with_source(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n495\t        self.source_path = Some(path.into());\n496\t        self\n497\t    }\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t/// Result of a YAML validation operation\n501\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n502\tpub struct ValidationResult {\n503\t    /// Whether validation passed\n504\t    pub valid: bool,\n505\t    /// List of validation errors\n506\t    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,\n507\t    /// List of validation warnings\n508\t    pub warnings: Vec<ValidationWarning>,\n509\t}\n510\t\n511\timpl ValidationResult {\n512\t    /// Create a successful validation result\n513\t    pub fn success() -> Self {\n514\t        Self {\n515\t            valid: true,\n516\t            errors: Vec::new(),\n517\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n518\t        }\n519\t    }\n520\t\n521\t    /// Create a failed validation result\n522\t    pub fn failure(errors: Vec<ValidationError>) -> Self {\n523\t        Self {\n524\t            valid: false,\n525\t            errors,\n526\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n527\t        }\n528\t    }\n529\t\n530\t    /// Check if validation passed\n531\t    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {\n532\t        self.valid && self.errors.is_empty()\n533\t    }\n534\t\n535\t    /// Check if there are any errors\n536\t    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {\n537\t        !self.errors.is_empty()\n538\t    }\n539\t\n540\t    /// Check if there are any warnings\n541\t    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n542\t        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n543\t    }\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\timpl Default for ValidationResult {\n547\t    fn default() -> Self {\n548\t        Self::success()\n549\t    }\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t/// A validation error\n553\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n554\tpub struct ValidationError {\n555\t    /// Path to the invalid element (e.g., \"server.port\")\n556\t    pub path: String,\n557\t    /// Error message\n558\t    pub message: String,\n559\t    /// Line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n560\t    pub line: Option<usize>,\n561\t    /// Indentation error type (if this is an indentation error)\n562\t    pub indentation_error_type: Option<crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::IndentationErrorType>,\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\timpl ValidationError {\n566\t    /// Create a new validation error\n567\t    pub fn new(path: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n568\t        Self {\n569\t            path: path.into(),\n570\t armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseWarning, ParseWarningKind};\n683\t    ///\n684\t    /// let warning = ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_api\", \"new_api\");\n685\t    /// assert!(matches!(warning.kind, }\n710\t}\n711\t\n712\timpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarning {\n713\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n714\t        let location = self.line.map_or_else(|| Clone)]\n721\tpub enum ParseWarningKind {\n722\t    /// A deprecated field was used\n723\t    ///\n724\t    /// Indicates that a field has been deprecated and should be replaced\n725\t    DeprecatedField {\n726\t        /// The deprecated field name\n727\t        old_field: String,\n728\t        /// The recommended replacement field\n729\t        new_field: String,\n730\t    },\n731\t\n732\t    /// An unknown key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n733\t    ///\n734\t    /// Indicates a key that was not recognized but was not rejected\n735\t    /// due to lenient parsing values\n741\t    DuplicateKey(String),\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\timpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarningKind {\n745\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n746\t deprecated, use '{}' instead\",\n751\t                    old_field, new_field\n752\t                )\n753\t            }\n754\t            Self::UnknownKey(key) => {\n755\t                write!(f, \"warning: unknown key '{}'\", key)\n756\t            }\n757\t            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => {\n758\t                write!(f, \"warning: duplicate key '{}'\", key)\n759\t            }\n760\t        }\n761\t    }\n762\t}\n763\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4d3293f3-57ae-426e-9a3e-e714b15a9914","uuid":"591f3218-ba48-49a8-8520-31c3107e0ef3","timestamp":"2026-07-12T09:17:02.773Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/types.rs","content":"//! Result types for YAML parsing operations\n//!\n//! This module defines the result types used by the YAML parser.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::error::{ParseError, Result};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Status enum representing success/error states for Result types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum Status {\n    /// Operation completed successfully\n    SUCCESS,\n    /// Operation encountered an error\n    ERROR,\n}\n\nimpl Status {\n    /// Check if status is SUCCESS\n    pub fn is_success(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Status::SUCCESS)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if status is ERROR\n    pub fn is_error(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Status::ERROR)\n    }\n\n    /// Convert from boolean (true = SUCCESS, false = ERROR)\n    pub fn from_bool(success: bool) -> Self {\n        if success {\n            Status::SUCCESS\n        } else {\n            Status::ERROR\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to boolean\n    pub fn as_bool(self) -> bool {\n        self.is_success()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Status {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Status::SUCCESS => write!(f, \"SUCCESS\"),\n            Status::ERROR => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML operation with status, data, and error fields\n///\n/// This is a simple dataclass-style result type that holds:\n/// - `status`: A Status enum indicating SUCCESS or ERROR\n/// - `data`: Optional generic data payload\n/// - `error`: Optional error message string\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct OperationResult<T> {\n    /// The operation status (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n    pub status: Status,\n    /// The parsed content data, if successful\n    pub data: Option<T>,\n    /// The error message, if the operation failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> OperationResult<T> {\n    /// Create a new OperationResult with all three fields\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `status` - The Status enum value (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n    /// * `data` - Optional data payload\n    /// * `error` - Optional error message\n    pub fn new(status: Status, data: Option<T>, error: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self { status, data, error }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a successful OperationResult with data\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `data` - The successful result data\n    pub fn success(data: T) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status: Status::SUCCESS,\n            data: Some(data),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed OperationResult with an error message\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `message` - The error message describing the failure\n    pub fn error(message: String) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status: Status::ERROR,\n            data: None,\n            error: Some(message),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation was successful\n    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_success()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation failed\n    pub fn is_error(&self) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_error()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a reference to the data, if successful\n    pub fn get_data(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n        self.data.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the error message as a string slice, if failed\n    pub fn get_error(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n        self.error.as_deref()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML parsing operation\n///\n/// `ParseResult<T>` represents the outcome of a YAML parsing operation, encapsulating\n/// either a successful parse with its associated data and metadata, or a failure with\n/// detailed error information.\n///\n/// # Type Parameter\n///\n/// - `T` - The type of the parsed value. This is typically the target type that the YAML\n///   content is being parsed into (e.g., a configuration struct, `serde_yaml::Value`,\n///   or any deserializable type).\n///\n/// # Fields\n///\n/// ## Core Fields\n///\n/// - `value: Option<T>` - The successfully parsed value. Present when parsing succeeds.\n/// - `error: Option<ParseError>` - Detailed error information. Present when parsing fails.\n/// - `metadata: ParseMetadata` - Metadata about the parsing operation (lines processed,\n///   bytes processed, processing time, source path). Always present.\n/// - `warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>` - Collection of non-fatal warnings that occurred during\n///   parsing. Parsing can succeed with warnings present.\n///\n/// # Design Philosophy\n///\n/// `ParseResult<T>` follows a structured approach to representing parsing outcomes:\n///\n/// 1. **Explicit success/failure states** - Success requires both `value` present and\n///    `error` absent. Failure requires `error` present and `value` absent.\n/// 2. **Rich context** - Both success and failure carry metadata about the operation.\n/// 3. **Warnings without failure** - Warnings are non-fatal; parsing can succeed with\n///    warnings present (e.g., deprecated field usage, unknown keys in lenient mode).\n/// 4. **Composable operations** - Supports `map()` for transforming success values,\n///    and `From<Result<T>>` for interoperability with standard `Result` types.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ## Successful Parse\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseMetadata};\n///\n/// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n/// assert!(result.is_success());\n/// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n/// assert!(result.warnings().is_empty());\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Failed Parse\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n///\n/// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n/// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n/// assert!(result.is_failure());\n/// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Parse with Warnings\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n///\n/// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n/// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_field\", \"new_field\"));\n///\n/// assert!(result.is_success());\n/// assert!(!result.warnings().is_empty());\n/// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Mapping Over Success\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n///\n/// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n/// let mapped = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n///\n/// assert_eq!(mapped.value(), Some(&20));\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseResult<T> {\n    /// The parsed value, if successful\n    value: Option<T>,\n    /// The error, if parsing failed\n    error: Option<ParseError>,\n    /// Additional metadata about the parse operation\n    metadata: ParseMetadata,\n    /// Non-fatal warnings that occurred during parsing\n    warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> ParseResult<T> {\n    /// Create a successful parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `value` - The successfully parsed value\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A `ParseResult` with the value set, no error, empty warnings, and default metadata\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n    ///\n    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// assert!(result.is_success());\n    /// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn success(value: T) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            value: Some(value),\n            error: None,\n            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `error` - The error that caused parsing to fail\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A `ParseResult` with the error set, no value, empty warnings, and default metadata\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n    /// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n    /// assert!(result.is_failure());\n    /// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn failure(error: ParseError) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            value: None,\n            error: Some(error),\n            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parse was successful\n    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.error.is_none() && self.value.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parse failed\n    pub fn is_failure(&self) -> bool {\n        self.error.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the parsed value\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if the parse failed\n    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n        self.value.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the error, if any\n    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&ParseError> {\n        self.error.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the metadata for this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the `ParseMetadata` containing information about lines processed,\n    /// bytes processed, processing time, and source path\n    pub fn metadata(&self) -> &ParseMetadata {\n        &self.metadata\n    }\n\n    /// Get the warnings for this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A slice of `ParseWarning` items representing non-fatal issues that occurred during parsing\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n    ///\n    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old\", \"new\"));\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn warnings(&self) -> &[ParseWarning] {\n        &self.warnings\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this parse result has any warnings\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// `true` if there are warnings present, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Add a warning to this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `warning` - The warning to add\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n    ///\n    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"deprecated_field\"));\n    /// assert!(result.has_warnings());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_warning(&mut self, warning: ParseWarning) {\n        self.warnings.push(warning);\n    }\n\n    /// Add warnings to this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `warnings` - An iterator of warnings to add\n    pub fn add_warnings<I>(&mut self, warnings: I)\n    where\n        I: IntoIterator<Item = ParseWarning>,\n    {\n        self.warnings.extend(warnings);\n    }\n\n    /// Set the metadata for this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `metadata` - The metadata to set\n    pub fn with_metadata(mut self, metadata: ParseMetadata) -> Self {\n        self.metadata = metadata;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Unwrap the value, consuming the result\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    /// Panics if the parse failed (i.e., if `error` is present)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n    ///\n    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), 42);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn unwrap(self) -> T {\n        self.value.expect(\n            \"called unwrap() on a failed ParseResult\"\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Unwrap the value or return a default\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `default` - The default value to return if parsing failed\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parsed value if successful, or the provided default\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n    ///\n    /// let success: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// assert_eq!(success.unwrap_or(0), 42);\n    ///\n    /// let failure = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(ParseError::syntax(\"error\"));\n    /// assert_eq!(failure.unwrap_or(0), 0);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T {\n        self.value.unwrap_or(default)\n    }\n\n    /// Map the success value to a new type\n    ///\n    /// # Type Parameters\n    /// * `U` - The target type after mapping\n    /// * `F` - Function type that transforms `T` into `U`\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `f` - A function that transforms the success value\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A new `ParseResult<U>` with the transformed value, preserving error, metadata, and warnings\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n    ///\n    /// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n    /// let doubled = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(doubled.value(), Some(&20));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn map<U, F>(self, f: F) -> ParseResult<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n    {\n        match self.value {\n            Some(v) => ParseResult {\n                value: Some(f(v)),\n                error: None,\n                metadata: self.metadata,\n                warnings: self.warnings,\n            },\n            None => ParseResult {\n                value: None,\n                error: self.error,\n                metadata: self.metadata,\n                warnings: self.warnings,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<T> From<Result<T>> for ParseResult<T> {\n    fn from(result: Result<T>) -> Self {\n        match result {\n            Ok(value) => Self::success(value),\n            Err(error) => Self::failure(error),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Metadata about a parsing operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ParseMetadata {\n    /// Number of lines processed\n    pub lines_processed: usize,\n    /// Number of bytes processed\n    pub bytes_processed: usize,\n    /// Processing time in nanoseconds\n    pub processing_time_ns: Option<u64>,\n    /// Source file path, if known\n    pub source_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseMetadata {\n    /// Create new metadata\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Set the number of lines processed\n    pub fn with_lines(mut self, lines: usize) -> Self {\n        self.lines_processed = lines;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the number of bytes processed\n    pub fn with_bytes(mut self, bytes: usize) -> Self {\n        self.bytes_processed = bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the source path\n    pub fn with_source(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.source_path = Some(path.into());\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML validation operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidationResult {\n    /// Whether validation passed\n    pub valid: bool,\n    /// List of validation errors\n    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,\n    /// List of validation warnings\n    pub warnings: Vec<ValidationWarning>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationResult {\n    /// Create a successful validation result\n    pub fn success() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            valid: true,\n            errors: Vec::new(),\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed validation result\n    pub fn failure(errors: Vec<ValidationError>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            valid: false,\n            errors,\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if validation passed\n    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {\n        self.valid && self.errors.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any errors\n    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.errors.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any warnings\n    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ValidationResult {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::success()\n    }\n}\n\n/// A validation error\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidationError {\n    /// Path to the invalid element (e.g., \"server.port\")\n    pub path: String,\n    /// Error message\n    pub message: String,\n    /// Line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n    /// Indentation error type (if this is an indentation error)\n    pub indentation_error_type: Option<crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::IndentationErrorType>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationError {\n    /// Create a new validation error\n    pub fn new(path: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            path: path.into(),\n            message: message.into(),\n            line: None,\n            indentation_error_type: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this error\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the indentation error type for this error\n    pub fn with_indentation_error_type(mut self, error_type: crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::IndentationErrorType) -> Self {\n        self.indentation_error_type = Some(error_type);\n        self\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ValidationError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match &self.line {\n            Some(line) => write!(f, \"{}: validation error at '{}': {}\", line, self.path, self.message),\n            None => write!(f, \"validation error at '{}': {}\", self.path, self.message),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ValidationError {}\n\n/// A validation warning\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidationWarning {\n    /// Path to the element that triggered the warning\n    pub path: String,\n    /// Warning message\n    pub message: String,\n    /// Line number where the warning occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationWarning {\n    /// Create a new validation warning\n    pub fn new(path: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            path: path.into(),\n            message: message.into(),\n            line: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this warning\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ValidationWarning {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match &self.line {\n            Some(line) => write!(f, \"{}: warning at '{}': {}\", line, self.path, self.message),\n            None => write!(f, \"warning at '{}': {}\", self.path, self.message),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A non-fatal warning that occurred during parsing\n///\n/// `ParseWarning` represents issues that don't prevent parsing from completing\n/// but may indicate deprecated usage, potential problems, or other concerns.\n///\n/// # Warning Types\n///\n/// - `DeprecatedField` - A field that has been deprecated and should be migrated\n/// - `UnknownKey` - An unknown key encountered (only in lenient mode)\n/// - `DuplicateKey` - A duplicate key that was handled (lenient mode)\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseWarning {\n    /// The kind of warning\n    pub kind: ParseWarningKind,\n    /// Line number where the warning occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseWarning {\n    /// Create a new parse warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `kind` - The type of warning\n    pub fn new(kind: ParseWarningKind) -> Self {\n        Self { kind, line: None }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `line` - The line number (1-indexed)\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Create a deprecated field warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `old_field` - The deprecated field name\n    /// * `new_field` - The recommended replacement field\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseWarning, ParseWarningKind};\n    ///\n    /// let warning = ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_api\", \"new_api\");\n    /// assert!(matches!(warning.kind, ParseWarningKind::DeprecatedField { .. }));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn deprecated_field(old_field: impl Into<String>, new_field: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseWarningKind::DeprecatedField {\n            old_field: old_field.into(),\n            new_field: new_field.into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Create an unknown key warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `key` - The unknown key name\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseWarning, ParseWarningKind};\n    ///\n    /// let warning = ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"unknown_setting\");\n    /// assert!(matches!(warning.kind, ParseWarningKind::UnknownKey(_)));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn unknown_key(key: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseWarningKind::UnknownKey(key.into()))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarning {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        let location = self.line.map_or_else(|| \"<unknown>\".to_string(), |l| l.to_string());\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n    }\n}\n\n/// The kind of parse warning\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum ParseWarningKind {\n    /// A deprecated field was used\n    ///\n    /// Indicates that a field has been deprecated and should be replaced\n    DeprecatedField {\n        /// The deprecated field name\n        old_field: String,\n        /// The recommended replacement field\n        new_field: String,\n    },\n\n    /// An unknown key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n    ///\n    /// Indicates a key that was not recognized but was not rejected\n    /// due to lenient parsing mode\n    UnknownKey(String),\n\n    /// A duplicate key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n    ///\n    /// Indicates a duplicate key that was handled by taking one of the values\n    DuplicateKey(String),\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarningKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::DeprecatedField { old_field, new_field } => {\n                write!(\n                    f,\n                    \"warning: field '{}' is deprecated, use '{}' instead\",\n                    old_field, new_field\n                )\n            }\n            Self::UnknownKey(key) => {\n                write!(f, \"warning: unknown key '{}'\", key)\n            }\n            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => {\n                write!(f, \"warning: duplicate key '{}'\", key)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":763,"startLine":1,"totalLines":763}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_38e7ab7550884ce99694fcba","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Error types for YAML parsing\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the error types used throughout the YAML parser.\n4\t//!\n5\t//! # Error Handling Philosophy\n6\t//!\n7\t//! The `ParseError` type follows a structured approach to error handling that emphasizes:\n8\t//!\n9\t//! 1. **Clear categorization** - Each error falls into a distinct category (syntax, I/O, validation, etc.)\n10\t//! 2. **Rich context** - Errors carry location information, context messages, and code snippets\n11\t//! 3. **Composability** - Errors propagate cleanly through the call stack using Rust's `?` operator\n12\t//! 4. **User-friendly output** - Multiple formatting options for different use cases (logging, UI, debugging)\n13\t//!\n14\t//! ## When to Use Each Variant\n15\t//!\n16\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Io` vs Other Variants\n17\t//!\n18\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`] for **file system and I/O operation failures**:\n19\t//! - File not found (`std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound`)\n20\t//! - Permission denied (`std::io::ErrorKind::PermissionDenied`)\n21\t//! - Read/write failures (`std::io::ErrorKind::Other`)\n22\t//! - Network I/O errors (if applicable)\n23\t//!\n24\t//! **Do not use** `Io` for:\n25\t//! - YAML syntax errors → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n26\t//! - Type mismatches → use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n27\t//! - Constraint violations → use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`]\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ```ignore\n30\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Io for file system errors\n31\t//! let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → Io\n32\t//!\n33\t//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use Io for parsing errors\n34\t//! // let error = ParseError::io(\"invalid YAML structure\");  // Wrong! Use Syntax instead\n35\t//! ```\n36\t//!\n37\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8` vs Other Variants\n38\t//!\n39\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`] specifically for **encoding errors**:\n40\t//! - Invalid UTF-8 byte sequences in input\n41\t//! - Failed UTF-8 validation when converting bytes to strings\n42\t//!\n43\t//! **Do not use** `InvalidUtf8` for:\n44\t//! - I/O errors during file reading → use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n45\t//! - General parsing failures → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n46\t//!\n47\t//! ```ignore\n48\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use InvalidUtf8 for encoding errors\n49\t//! let string = String::from_utf8(bytes)?;  // FromUtf8Error → InvalidUtf8\n50\t//!\n51\t//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use InvalidUtf8 for file reading errors\n52\t//! // let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8);  // Wrong! Use Io instead\n53\t//! ```\n54\t//!\n55\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof` vs `ParseErrorKind::Syntax`\n56\t//!\n57\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof`] when **input ends prematurely**:\n58\t//! - Incomplete YAML documents (missing closing brackets, braces, quotes)\n59\t//! - Truncated files or streams\n60\t//! - Multi-document YAML streams that end mid-document\n61\t//!\n62\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`] for **general YAML syntax violations**:\n63\t//! - Invalid indentation\n64\t//! - Invalid escape sequences\n65\t//! - Malformed scalars or mappings\n66\t//! - Any YAML grammar violation that's not specifically EOF-related\n67\t//!\n68\t//! ```ignore\n69\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use UnexpectedEof for incomplete input\n70\t//! if input.ends_with(\"key: \") {\n71\t//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n72\t//! }\n73\t//!\n74\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Syntax for general YAML errors\n75\t//! if !is_valid_indentation(line) {\n76\t//!     return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\"));\n77\t//! }\n78\t//! ```\n79\t//!\n80\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n81\t//!\n82\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`] when **a value has the wrong Rust/YAML type**:\n83\t//! - Expecting an integer but finding a string\n84\t//! - Expecting a sequence but finding a scalar\n85\t//! - Expecting a boolean but finding a number\n86\t//!\n87\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **semantic constraint violations**:\n88\t//! - Value out of allowed range (e.g., port number > 65535)\n89\t//! - String doesn't match required pattern (e.g., invalid email format)\n90\t//! - Array length constraints violated\n91\t//! - Business logic or schema validation failures\n92\t//!\n93\t//! The key distinction: `TypeMismatch` is about **type**, `Validation` is about **value**.\n94\t//!\n95\t//! ```ignore\n96\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use TypeMismatch for type errors\n97\t//! let port = value[\"port\"].as_i64()\n98\t//!     .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\"))?;\n99\t//!\n100\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for value constraints\n101\t//! if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n102\t//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n103\t//! }\n104\t//! ```\n105\t//!\n106\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n107\t//!\n108\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey`] specifically for **duplicate key errors**:\n109\t//! - YAML mappings with repeated keys (YAML 1.2 spec violation)\n110\t//! - Configuration files with conflicting entries\n111\t//!\n112\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **general validation failures** including:\n113\t//! - Missing required fields (not duplicated, but absent)\n114\t//! - Inter-field constraint violations (e.g., \"start_time > end_time\")\n115\t//! - Schema validation failures beyond type/duplicate checking\n116\t//!\n117\t//! ```ignore\n118\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use DuplicateKey for duplicate keys\n119\t//! if keys.contains(&key) {\n120\t//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(key)));\n121\t//! }\n122\t//!\n123\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for missing required fields\n124\t//! if value[\"name\"].is_null() {\n125\t//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: 'name'\"));\n126\t//! }\n127\t//! ```\n128\t//!\n129\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Other` (Catch-all) vs Specific Variants\n130\t//!\n131\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Other`] **only as a last resort** for errors that don't fit other categories:\n132\t//! - Unclassifiable serde_yaml errors\n133\t//! - Errors from external libraries that don't map cleanly\n134\t//! - Temporary error cases during refactoring\n135\t//!\n136\t//! **Prefer specific variants** whenever possible:\n137\t//! - Unknown anchors → [`ParseErrorKind::UnknownAnchor`]\n138\t//! - Syntax errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n139\t//! - I/O errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n140\t//! - Type errors → [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n141\t//!\n142\t//! If you find yourself using `Other` frequently, consider adding a new specific variant.\n143\t//!\n144\t//! ```ignore\n145\t//! // ✅ Prefer specific variants\n146\t//! return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\"));\n147\t//!\n148\t//! // ⚠️ Use Other only as a catch-all\n149\t//! return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Other(\"unclassified error\".to_string())));\n150\t//! ```\n151\t//!\n152\t//! # Error Propagation Strategy\n153\t//!\n154\t//! The `ParseError` type is designed to work seamlessly with Rust's error propagation\n155\t//! mechanisms. It implements `From` for common error types, enabling the use of the `?`\n156\t//! operator for automatic error conversion.\n157\t//!\n158\t//! ## Basic Propagation with `?`\n159\t//!\n160\t//! ```ignore\n161\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n162\t//!\n163\t//! fn parse_config(path: &Path) -> Result<Config> {\n164\t//!     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → ParseError\n165\t//!     parse_yaml(&content)\n166\t//! }\n167\t//! ```\n168\t//!\n169\t//! ## Adding Context with `.context()`\n170\t//!\n171\t//! Use the builder-style `.context()` method to add contextual information:\n172\t//!\n173\t//! ```ignore\n174\t//! fn parse_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n175\t//!     let port = value[\"port\"]\n176\t//!         .as_i64()\n177\t//!         .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"null\"))\n178\t//!         .context(\"while parsing database configuration\")?;\n179\t//!\n180\t//!     Ok(DatabaseConfig { port })\n181\t//! }\n182\t//! ```\n183\t//!\n184\t//! ## Converting from Other Error Types\n185\t//!\n186\t//! `ParseError` implements `From` for:\n187\t//! - `std::io::Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n188\t//! - `serde_yaml::Error` → Appropriate [`ParseErrorKind`] based on error classification\n189\t//! - `std::str::Utf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n190\t//! - `std::string::FromUtf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n191\t//!\n192\t//! ## Custom Error Creation\n193\t//!\n194\t//! For domain-specific errors, create them directly:\n195\t//!\n196\t//! ```ignore\n197\t//! fn validate_port(port: i64) -> Result<u16> {\n198\t//!     if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n199\t//!         return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n200\t//!     }\n201\t//!     Ok(port as u16)\n202\t//! }\n203\t//! ```\n204\t//!\n205\t//! # Examples\n206\t//!\n207\t//! ## Basic Error Creation\n208\t//!\n209\t//! Creating errors using convenience constructors:\n210\t//!\n211\t//! ```\n212\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n213\t//!\n214\t//! // Create a syntax error\n215\t//! let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML indentation\");\n216\t//! assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n217\t//!\n218\t//! // Create a validation error\n219\t//! let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\");\n220\t//! assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n221\t//!\n222\t//! // Create a type mismatch error\n223\t//! let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n224\t//! assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n225\t//! assert_eq!(type_err.summary(), \"<unknown>: type mismatch at 'port': expected integer, got string\");\n226\t//! ```\n227\t//!\n228\t//! ## Error Propagation with `?`\n229\t//!\n230\t//! Using the `?` operator to automatically convert errors:\n231\t//!\n232\t//! ```\n233\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n234\t//! use std::fs;\n235\t//!\n236\t//! fn read_config(path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n237\t//!     // io::Error is automatically converted to ParseError via From impl\n238\t//!     let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n239\t//!     Ok(content)\n240\t//! }\n241\t//!\n242\t//! // This function demonstrates automatic error conversion\n243\t//! fn parse_config_size(path: &str) -> Result<usize> {\n244\t//!     let content = read_config(path)?;  // ParseError propagates via ?\n245\t//!     Ok(content.len())\n246\t//! }\n247\t//!\n248\t//! // Example: successful read\n249\t//! assert!(parse_config_size(\"/dev/null\").is_ok());\n250\t//! ```\n251\t//!\n252\t//! ## Custom Error Handling with Builder Pattern\n253\t//!\n254\t//! Using builder methods to add context and location information:\n255\t//!\n256\t//! ```\n257\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n258\t//!\n259\t//! fn validate_service_config(yaml_content: &str) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n260\t//!     // Simulating an error during validation\n261\t//!     if yaml_content.contains(\"port: abc\") {\n262\t//!         let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"service.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n263\t//!             .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\")\n264\t//!             .with_line(5)\n265\t//!             .with_column(10)\n266\t//!             .with_context(\"while validating service configuration\")\n267\t//!             .with_snippet(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\");\n268\t//!\n269\t//!         return Err(error);\n270\t//!     }\n271\t//!     Ok(())\n272\t//! }\n273\t//!\n274\t//! // Create an error to verify the builder pattern\n275\t//! let error = ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\")\n276\t//!     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n277\t//!     .with_line(10)\n278\t//!     .with_column(5)\n279\t//!     .with_context(\"while parsing configuration\");\n280\t//!\n281\t//! assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n282\t//! assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n283\t//! assert_eq!(error.column, Some(5));\n284\t//! assert_eq!(error.context, \"while parsing configuration\");\n285\t//! ```\n286\t//!\n287\t//! ## Error Display and Formatting\n288\t//!\n289\t//! Different ways to format and display errors:\n290\t//!\n291\t//! ```\n292\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n293\t//!\n294\t//! // Create an error with full context\n295\t//! let error = ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected colon\")\n296\t//!     .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n297\t//!     .with_line(15)\n298\t//!     .with_column(8)\n299\t//!     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\")\n300\t//!     .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  host: localhost\\n  port: 5432:\\n    invalid\");\n301\t//!\n302\t//! // Get a single-line summary (good for logging)\n303\t//! let summary = error.summary();\n304\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15\"));\n305\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n306\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n307\t//!\n308\t//! // Get a detailed multi-line report (good for user display)\n309\t//! let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n310\t//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n311\t//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n312\t//!\n313\t//! // Get a structured representation (good for debugging)\n314\t//! let structured = error.format_structured();\n315\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n316\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15:8\"));\n317\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(15)\"));\n318\t//!\n319\t//! // Display implementation provides user-friendly output\n320\t//! let display_string = format!(\"{}\", error);\n321\t//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"config/database.yaml\"));\n322\t//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n323\t//! ```\n324\t//!\n325\t//! ## Error Conversion from Standard Types\n326\t//!\n327\t//! Automatic conversion from standard library errors:\n328\t//!\n329\t//! ```\n330\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n331\t//! use std::io;\n332\t//!\n333\t//! // io::Error converts to ParseError::Io\n334\t//! let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n335\t//! let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n336\t//! assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n337\t//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"I/O error\"));\n338\t//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"file not found\"));\n339\t//!\n340\t//! // String::from_utf8 Error converts to ParseError::InvalidUtf8\n341\t//! let invalid_bytes = b\"\\xff\\xfe\\xfd\";\n342\t//! let utf8_err = String::from_utf8(invalid_bytes.to_vec()).unwrap_err();\n343\t//! let parse_err: ParseError = utf8_err.into();\n344\t//! assert!(matches!(parse_err.kind, armor::parsers::yaml::ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8));\n345\t//! ```\n346\t//!\n347\t//! ## Working with Error Types\n348\t//!\n349\t//! Checking error types and handling specific cases:\n350\t//!\n351\t//! ```\n352\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n353\t//!\n354\t//! let errors = vec![\n355\t//!     ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\"),\n356\t//!     ParseError::io(\"file not found\"),\n357\t//!     ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\"),\n358\t//!     ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"integer\"),\n359\t//! ];\n360\t//!\n361\t//! // Check specific error types\n362\t//! assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n363\t//! assert!(errors[1].is_io());\n364\t//! assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n365\t//! assert!(errors[3].is_type_mismatch());\n366\t//!\n367\t//! // Filter errors by type\n368\t//! let syntax_errors: Vec<_> = errors.iter()\n369\t//!     .filter(|e| e.is_syntax())\n370\t//!     .collect();\n371\t//! assert_eq!(syntax_errors.len(), 1);\n372\t//! ```\n373\t\n374\tuse std::fmt;\n375\t\n376\t/// Main error type for YAML parsing operations\n377\t#[derive(Clone)]\n378\tpub struct ParseError {\n379\t    /// The kind of error that occurred\n380\t    pub kind: ParseErrorKind,\n381\t    /// The line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n382\t    pub line: Option<usize>,\n383\t    /// The column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n384\t    pub column: Option<usize>,\n385\t    /// The file or source path where the error occurred\n386\t    pub path: Option<String>,\n387\t    /// A code snippet showing the problematic segment\n388\t    pub snippet: Option<String>,\n389\t    /// Additional context about the error\n390\t    pub context: String,\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\timpl ParseError {\n394\t    /// Create a new ParseError with the given kind\n395\t    ///\n396\t    /// This is the base constructor for creating a ParseError. All fields except\n397\t    /// `kind` are set to their default values (None or empty). Use the builder methods\n398\t    /// to add additional context and location information.\n399\t    ///\n400\t    /// # Examples\n401\t    ///\n402\t    /// ```\n403\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind};\n404\t    ///\n405\t    /// let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof);\n406\t    /// assert!(matches!(error.kind, ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n407\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, None);\n408\t    /// ```\n409\t    ///\n410\t    /// For common error types, prefer using the convenience constructors:\n411\t    /// - [`ParseError::syntax()`] for syntax errors\n412\t    /// - [`ParseError::io()`] for I/O errors\n413\t    /// - [`ParseError::validation()`] for validation errors\n414\t    /// - [`ParseError::type_mismatch()`] for type mismatch errors\n415\t    pub fn new(kind: ParseErrorKind) -> Self {\n416\t        Self {\n417\t            kind,\n418\t            line: None,\n419\t            column: None,\n420\t            path: None,\n421\t            snippet: None,\n422\t            context: String::new(),\n423\t        }\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    /// Set the line number for this error\n427\t    ///\n428\t    /// Line numbers are 1-indexed, matching typical text editor conventions.\n429\t    ///\n430\t    /// # Examples\n431\t    ///\n432\t    /// ```\n433\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n434\t    ///\n435\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n436\t    ///     .with_line(42);\n437\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(42));\n438\t    /// ```\n439\t    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n440\t        self.line = Some(line);\n441\t        self\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    /// Set the column number for this error\n445\t    ///\n446\t    /// Column numbers are 1-indexed, indicating the character position within the line.\n447\t    ///\n448\t    /// # Examples\n449\t    ///\n450\t    /// ```\n451\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n452\t    ///\n453\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n454\t    ///     .with_column(15);\n455\t    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(15));\n456\t    /// ```\n457\t    pub fn with_column(mut self, column: usize) -> Self {\n458\t        self.column = Some(column);\n459\t        self\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    /// Set the file/source path for this error\n463\t    ///\n464\t    /// The path can be absolute or relative. It's displayed in error messages to help\n465\t    /// users locate the source of the error.\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Examples\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// ```\n470\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n471\t    ///\n472\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\")\n473\t    ///     .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\");\n474\t    /// assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/services.yaml\".to_string()));\n475\t    /// ```\n476\t    pub fn with_path(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n477\t        self.path = Some(path.into());\n478\t        self\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    /// Set the code snippet for this error\n482\t    ///\n483\t    /// The snippet should contain the relevant lines of source code where the error occurred.\n484\t    /// This is displayed in detailed error reports to help users understand the context.\n485\t    ///\n486\t    /// # Examples\n487\t    ///\n488\t    /// ```\n489\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n492\t    ///     .with_line(5)\n493\t    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  name: web\\n  port: abc\");\n494\t    ///\n495\t    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n496\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"service:\"));\n497\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"port: abc\"));\n498\t    /// ```\n499\t    pub fn with_snippet(mut self, snippet: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n500\t        self.snippet = Some(snippet.into());\n501\t        self\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    /// Set the context message for this error\n505\t    ///\n506\t    /// Context provides additional information about what operation was being performed\n507\t    /// when the error occurred. This helps users understand the broader scenario.\n508\t    ///\n509\t    /// # Examples\n510\t    ///\n511\t    /// ```\n512\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n513\t    ///\n514\t    /// let error = ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n515\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\");\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// let summary = error.summary();\n518\t    /// assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n519\t    /// ```\n520\t    pub fn with_context(mut self, context: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n521\t        self.context = context.into();\n522\t        self\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    /// Set both line and column for this error\n526\t    ///\n527\t    /// Convenience method for setting location with a single call.\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// # Examples\n530\t    ///\n531\t    /// ```\n532\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n533\t    ///\n534\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n535\t    ///     .with_location(10, 25);\n536\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n537\t    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(25));\n538\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"10:25\");\n539\t    /// ```\n540\t    pub fn with_location(mut self, line: usize, column: usize) -> Self {\n541\t        self.line = Some(line);\n542\t        self.column = Some(column);\n543\t        self\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    /// Get a formatted location string (e.g., \"file.yaml:10:5\" or \"<unknown>:10\")\n547\t    ///\n548\t    /// This method generates a human-readable location string based on the available\n549\t    /// location information (path, line, column). The format adapts based on which fields are set.\n550\t    ///\n551\t    /// # Examples\n552\t    ///\n553\t    /// ```\n554\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n555\t    ///\n556\t    /// // Full location with path, line, and column\n557\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n558\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n559\t    ///     .with_location(10, 5);\n560\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml:10:5\");\n561\t    ///\n562\t    /// // Line and column only\n563\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n564\t    ///     .with_location(42, 15);\n565\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"42:15\");\n566\t    ///\n567\t    /// // Path only\n568\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n569\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\");\n570\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml\");\n571\t    ///\n572\t    /// // Unknown location\n573\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n574\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"<unknown>\");\n575\t    /// ```\n576\t    pub fn location_string(&self) -> String {\n577\t        match (&self.path, self.line, self.column) {\n578\t            (Some(path), Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}:{}\", path, line, col),\n579\t            (Some(path), Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}:{}\", path, line),\n580\t            (Some(path), None, Some(col)) => format!(\"{}::{}\", path, col),\n581\t            (Some(path), None, None) => path.clone(),\n582\t            (None, Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}\", line, col),\n583\t            (None, Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}\", line),\n584\t            (None, None, Some(col)) => format!(\"col {}\", col),\n585\t            (None, None, None) => \"<unknown>\".to_string(),\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    /// Get a brief summary of the error (single line, suitable for logging)\n590\t    ///\n591\t    /// The summary includes location, error kind, and context (if set). This is ideal\n592\t    /// for logging purposes where a compact, single-line error message is desired.\n593\t    ///\n594\t    /// # Examples\n595\t    ///\n596\t    /// ```\n597\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n598\t    ///\n599\t    /// // Summary with context\n600\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n601\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n602\t    ///     .with_line(10)\n603\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing services\");\n604\t    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n605\t    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token - while parsing services\");\n606\t    ///\n607\t    /// // Summary without context\n608\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n609\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n610\t    ///     .with_line(10);\n611\t    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n612\t    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token\");\n613\t    /// ```\n614\t    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n615\t        let location = self.location_string();\n616\t        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n617\t            format!(\"{}: {} - {}\", location, self.kind, self.context)\n618\t        } else {\n619\t            format!(\"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n620\t        }\n621\t    }\n622\t\n623\t    /// Get a detailed multi-line error report with snippet and visual indicator\n624\t    ///\n625\t    /// This method generates a comprehensive error report suitable for displaying\n626\t    /// to users. It includes the error summary, context (if set), and a code snippet\n627\t    /// with a visual indicator (^) pointing to the error location.\n628\t    ///\n629\t    /// # Examples\n630\t    ///\n631\t    /// ```\n632\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n633\t    ///\n634\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n635\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n636\t    ///     .with_line(5)\n637\t    ///     .with_column(10)\n638\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\")\n639\t    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  port: abc\");\n640\t    ///\n641\t    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n642\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"error:\"));\n643\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"config.yaml:5:10\"));\n644\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"syntax error: invalid port value\"));\n645\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"while parsing service configuration\"));\n646\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n647\t    /// ```\n648\t    pub fn detailed_report(&self) -> String {\n649\t        let mut report = String::new();\n650\t\n651\t        // Header line with location and error kind\n652\t        report.push_str(&format!(\"error: {}\\n\", self.summary()));\n653\t\n654\t        // Add context section if present\n655\t        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n656\t            report.push_str(&format!(\"  context: {}\\n\", self.context));\n657\t        }\n658\t\n659\t        // Add snippet with visual indicator\n660\t        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n661\t            report.push_str(\"\\n  snippet:\\n\");\n662\t            for line in snippet.lines() {\n663\t                report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}\\n\", line));\n664\t            }\n665\t\n666\t            // Add visual indicator for column position\n667\t            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n668\t                if col > 0 {\n669\t                    report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}^\\n\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1))));\n670\t                }\n671\t            }\n672\t        }\n673\t\n674\t        report\n675\t    }\n676\t\n677\t    /// Format this error as a structured log entry (JSON-like but readable)\n678\t    ///\n679\t    /// This method produces a structured representation of the error suitable for\n680\t    /// programmatic analysis or structured logging systems.\n681\t    ///\n682\t    /// # Examples\n683\t    ///\n684\t    /// ```\n685\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n686\t    ///\n687\t    /// let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n688\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n689\t    ///     .with_line(8)\n690\t    ///     .with_column(10);\n691\t    ///\n692\t    /// let formatted = error.format_structured();\n693\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n694\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"config.yaml:8:10\"));\n695\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n696\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"column: Some(10)\"));\n697\t    /// ```\n698\t    pub fn format_structured(&self) -> String {\n699\t        format!(\n700\t            \"ParseError {{ kind: {:?}, location: {}, line: {:?}, column: {:?} }}\",\n701\t            self.kind,\n702\t            self.location_string(),\n703\t            self.line,\n704\t            self.column\n705\t        )\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    /// Create a syntax error\n709\t    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n710\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Syntax(msg.into()))\n711\t    }\n712\t\n713\t    /// Create an I/O error\n714\t    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n715\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(msg.into()))\n716\t    }\n717\t\n718\t    /// Create a validation error\n719\t    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n720\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Validation(msg.into()))\n721\t    }\n722\t\n723\t    /// Create a type mismatch error\n724\t    ///\n725\t    /// # Arguments\n726\t    /// * `field` - The field path where the error occurred\n727\t    /// * `expected` - The expected type description\n728\t    /// * `actual` - The actual type that was received\n729\t    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n730\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch {\n731\t            field: field.into(),\n732\t            expected: expected.into(),\n733\t            actual: actual.into(),\n734\t        })\n735\t    }\n736\t\n737\t    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n738\t    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n739\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Syntax(_))\n740\t    }\n741\t\n742\t    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n743\t    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n744\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Io(_))\n745\t    }\n746\t\n747\t    /// Check if this is a validation error\n748\t    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n749\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Validation(_))\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n753\t    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n754\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch { .. })\n755\t    }\n756\t}\n757\t\n758\timpl fmt::Display for ParseError {\n759\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n760\t        // Use the summary method for concise display\n761\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())?;\n762\t\n763\t        // Add detailed section with snippet if available\n764\t        if let Some(snippet) =  {}\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1)))?;\n774\t                    write!(f, \"^\")?;\n775\t                }\n776\t            }\n777\t        }\n778\t\n779\t        Ok(())\n780\t    }\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\timpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n784\t\n785\timpl PartialEq for ParseError {\n786\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n787\t        // Only compare core error identification fields\n788\t        // Context and snippet are NOT included in equality\n789\t        self.kind == other.kind\n790\t            && self.line == other.line\n791\t            && self.column == other.column\n792\t            && self.path == other.path\n793\t    }\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\timpl fmt::Debug for ParseError {\n797\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) ->
